public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as inventory steward.
appearance: Sel Mere carries the marks of inventory steward in ink, rain, and polished cloth: practical wear, maintained details, and a look that suggests they expect to be read before they are trusted.
manner: Views problems as maintenance loads, throughput failures, or structural weaknesses before anything else. In Transpolity, that reads as someone boundary-crossing and adaptive, speaking to whichever audience or protocol is presently in front of them, and the habits of a inventory steward still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of inventory steward in and around Silkweaver’s Chapel, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Protect their footing in Silkweaver’s Chapel while steering current strain away from a public rupture they may not survive politically or materially.
hidden_pressure: A private obligation, debt, promise, or fear tied to reputational exposure is narrowing their options faster than they admit.

adventure_hooks.summary: Sel Mere needs outside help before wear, sabotage, or bottleneck pressure turns into a public failure in Silkweaver’s Chapel.
Working with Sel Mere can draw the party into crew discipline, resource shortage, or blame transfer around Silkweaver’s Chapel.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained.
public_signal: Tool wear, work scars, or system-focused habits make Sel Mere visibly responsible for keeping something larger from failing.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Silkweaver’s Chapel: worksites, depots, spans, loading points, maintenance corridors, or workshops

first_impression: Sel Mere presents as Views problems as maintenance loads, throughput failures, or structural weaknesses before anything else. In Transpolity, that reads as someone boundary-crossing and adaptive, speaking to whichever audience or protocol is presently in front of them, and the habits of a inventory steward still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention. and immediately reads for leverage, obligation, and risk.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for Inventory Steward, and proof that strangers will not create avoidable noise in Transpolity.
what_they_refuse: Sel Mere refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing Inventory Steward too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Sel Mere can open doors, explain local expectations, or redirect trouble before it reaches a harder authority layer.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Sel Mere adds delay, scrutiny, and reputational drag before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Sel Mere is Known locally through day-to-day work as inventory steward. and rarely acts without a second motive.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or mercy from a Inventory Steward.
social_texture: Sel Mere speaks like someone who has spent too long translating private stakes into acceptable public language. Around Silkweaver’s Chapel, people read them as socially alert, procedural, and sensitive to status.
money_pressure: Their material pressure is tied to favors and tariffs; even when wealthy, they think in terms of loss, leverage, and what one bad week would expose.
status_anxiety: What they fear most is not simple failure but reputational exposure becoming socially visible before they can contain it.
long_memory: They remember who made their work harder, who preserved dignity under strain, and who tried to force speed without bearing consequence.
obvious_tell: When pressure rises, Sel touches clothing, tools, or insignia before answering, as if checking that their role is still intact.
best_use_case: Use Sel Mere when the party needs a socially grounded face for Silkweaver’s Chapel: access, warning, delay, price, or reluctant help.
private_contradiction: They want to appear consistent, but their real choices are split between duty, self-protection, and preserving one relationship they cannot afford to lose.
